# tldrq.com App

> Save, organize, and track your reading with a minimal web app.

## Overview

A bookmark manager built with Next.js 16 (App Router), Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth), and Tailwind CSS v4. Users authenticate via Google or GitHub OAuth, save URLs with auto-fetched metadata, organize into collections, set priorities, and track read status.

## Architecture

- **Next.js 16 App Router** with server components for data fetching and client components for interactivity
- **Supabase** for PostgreSQL database, Row Level Security, and OAuth authentication
- **Tailwind CSS v4** with class-based dark mode (`@custom-variant dark`)
- **Cheerio** for server-side HTML parsing (OG metadata extraction)
- **Deployed on Vercel**

## Key Files

- `src/app/(main)/page.tsx` — Main dashboard (server component, fetches bookmarks/collections)
- `src/app/(main)/layout.tsx` — Authenticated layout with header
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — Root layout with inline theme script for flash-free dark mode
- `src/app/api/bookmarks/route.ts` — CRUD for bookmarks, auto-assigns YouTube/Vimeo/TikTok/Twitter URLs to collections
- `src/app/api/collections/route.ts` — CRUD for collections
- `src/app/auth/callback/route.ts` — OAuth callback, creates default collections (Videos, Tweets)
- `src/lib/utils/metadata.ts` — Fetches OG metadata; oEmbed for YouTube, Twitter/X, Vimeo, TikTok, Spotify, Reddit, SoundCloud, Flickr, SlideShare; OG scraping for Instagram
- `src/lib/utils/priority.ts` — Priority levels config (urgent, high, normal, low)
- `src/components/bookmarks/bookmark-card.tsx` — Main bookmark display with menus for move/priority/delete
- `src/components/layout/theme-toggle.tsx` — Dark/light mode toggle with localStorage persistence
- `src/types/database.ts` — TypeScript interfaces for Bookmark and Collection
- `supabase/schema.sql` — Combined database schema (tables, RLS policies, functions)

## Data Model

Three tables with Row Level Security:

- **collections**: id, user_id, name, is_public, share_slug, created_at
- **bookmarks**: id, user_id, collection_id, url, title, description, image_url, site_name, repo_stars, repo_forks, repo_language, is_read, priority, priority_order (computed), created_at
- **shared_views**: id, user_id, slug, title, collection_id, collection_uncategorized, filter, priority, sort, search, is_active, created_at

## Features

- OAuth login (Google, GitHub) via Supabase Auth
- Auto-fetched metadata (title, description, thumbnail) for saved URLs
- oEmbed support for: YouTube, Twitter/X, Vimeo, TikTok, Spotify, Reddit, SoundCloud, Flickr, SlideShare
- Instagram: enhanced OG scraping (no public oEmbed)
- Collections with auto-categorization (YouTube/Vimeo/TikTok → Videos, Twitter → Tweets)
- Priority system (urgent/high/normal/low) with color borders and sorting
- Read/unread tracking
- Collapsible filter panel (collection, read status, priority, sort)
- Dark mode default with light mode toggle, localStorage persistence
- Public collection sharing via slug URLs
- Shared filtered views with custom titles and search
- Export displayed URLs to text file
- User profile display (name, avatar from OAuth provider)

## Environment Variables

```
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
```

## Commands

```
npm run dev    # Development server
npm run build  # Production build
npm start      # Start production server
npm run lint   # ESLint
```
